goal: Escalate the worst lead-time exposure on Columbia-class with a rationale.

check.rationale_text: Allegheny Avionics Group represents the worst lead-time exposure across all Columbia-class suppliers at 395 days — over 13 months. Compounding the schedule risk: it is a Tier 1 single-source supplier with no identified alternate, meaning any disruption directly impacts the critical path with no fallback. Foreign ownership stands at 38% (Germany), elevating supply-chain security concern. Risk score of 77 confirms this is a high-severity case requiring senior review and contingency planning (e.g., safety-stock build, qualification of an alternate source, or contractual delivery guarantees).

final_text:
I'll start by loading the triage tools and then investigate the Columbia-class suppliers.Now let me query Columbia-class suppliers sorted by lead time (descending) to find the worst exposure.The worst lead-time exposure is clear: **S-015 Allegheny Avionics Group** at **395 days** — a Tier 1, single-source supplier with 38% German ownership and a risk score of 77. I'll escalate it now.**Done.** Here's a summary of what I did and why:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Supplier | Allegheny Avionics Group (S-015) |
| Decision | **Escalate** |
| Lead Time | **395 days** — longest on Columbia-class by 71 days |
| Tier / Single-Source | Tier 1 / Yes — no alternate supplier |
| Foreign Ownership | 38% (Germany) |
| Risk Score | 77 |

The combination of a **395-day lead time**, **single-source Tier 1 criticality**, and **38% foreign ownership** makes S-015 the clear worst exposure on the program. An escalation is warranted so decision-makers can assess safety-stock options, accelerate alternate-source qualification, or negotiate contractual delivery protections.

judge.reason: Every claim maps precisely to the ground-truth fields (395-day lead time, Columbia-class, Tier 1, singleSource, 38% German ownership, riskScore 77), the reasoning is specific and causally linked, and no facts are invented.
